deck: Dr. Girma Tadesse of JSI traces Ethiopia's decades-long path toward a fully paperless community health system, and what it takes to move from collecting data to truly using it.
seo_description: Dr. Girma Tadesse of JSI on Ethiopia's eCHIS program and its journey toward a fully paperless community health system built on government ownership.
og_description: Dr. Girma Tadesse on Ethiopia's eCHIS program and its path toward a fully paperless community health system.

pull_quote.text: Our ultimate goal is not to see station worker having a tablet, fancy tablet, and playing with that. No. Our ultimate goal is to improve the health of the community.
pull_quote.cite: Dr. Girma Tadesse, on what paperless is really for

bullets: A reform decades in the making, from Ethiopia's 1991 government change to the 2016 information revolution that reshaped how health data is used
Inside eCHIS, the electronic Community Health Information System that automates 18 health service packages for health extension workers
Why CommCare was chosen: open source, offline first, and flexible enough for the government to own and customize locally
Closing the data loop, from household registration in the field to a national Apache Superset dashboard refreshed within hours
The ecosystem around it, integrating SIM Prints biometrics, DHIS2 reporting, and performance-based incentives for health workers
The road to paperless, the ambitious push to make selected woredas fully digital and the infrastructure, tablet, and connectivity hurdles in the way
Advice for other governments, start with a clear health vision, secure leadership commitment, and invest long term in local capacity

stats.label: health service packages delivered by health extension workers, now digitized in eCHIS
health posts reached so far, more than a third of the agrarian total
health posts Ethiopia is expected to have nationwide

guests.name: Dr. Girma Tadesse
guests.role: eCHIS Project Manager, a physician and health information systems expert with more than 25 years in Ethiopia's health system.
